steps.title: Distribute and combine like terms
steps.content: First multiply the two binomials, then multiply by $2$:$$\left(x-\tfrac{3}{2}\right)(x+14)=x^2+\tfrac{25}{2}x-21$$$$2\left(x^2+\tfrac{25}{2}x-21\right)=2x^2+25x-42$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The simplified expression is $2x^2+25x-42$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
